title: The consequences of relying on AI for accurate news
summary: A Media Lab study shows that, much like how GPS has weakened our navigation skills, AI can make us worse at detecting fake news.
author: Adam Conner-Simons | MIT Media Lab

title: The crucial human component in computing and AI
summary: The MIT Ethics of Computing Research Symposium brought together experts and researchers working at the heart of ethical and social impact in technology.
author: Amanda Diehl | MIT Schwarzman College of Computing

title: PATH to boost AI training and career opportunities for industry-aligned jobs
summary: MIT RAISE and Georgia State University announce an initiative to connect universities, community colleges, industry, and government to expand industry-aligned AI training and career pathways.
author: Media Lab

title: NSF renews support for MIT-led AI and physics institute, expanding a new model for discovery
summary: IAIFI enters its second phase with increased funding, broader ambitions, and a growing community at the frontier of AI and fundamental physics.
author: Laboratory for Nuclear Science

title: Teaching AI agents to ask better questions by playing “Battleship”
summary: MIT researchers use the classic game as a test bed for AI agents, finding a small AI model can outperform the biggest ones at 1 percent of the cost.
author: Alex Shipps | MIT CSAIL

title: Tod Machover receives George Peabody Medal for contributions to music and technology
summary: The George Peabody Medal is the highest honor bestowed by the Peabody Institute of the Johns Hopkins University.
author: Media Lab

title: MIT researchers teach AI models to interpret charts
summary: The new ChartNet training dataset could improve the accuracy of vision-language models that help analyze business trends or interpret scientific figures.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: Media Advisory: MIT to establish regional quantum hub
summary: With $25 million investment from the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, MIT to build a new shared-use facility to serve as a statewide quantum toolbox.

title: Technology usually creates jobs for young, skilled workers. Will AI do the same?
summary: A new study of the postwar U.S. shows which kinds of workers historically filled new tech-enabled jobs.
author: Peter Dizikes | MIT News

title: Building AI models that understand chemical principles
summary: Connor Coley works at the interface of chemistry and machine learning, to discover and design new drug compounds.
author: Anne Trafton | MIT News

title: Justin Solomon appointed associate dean of engineering education
summary: MIT faculty member in electrical engineering and computer science to focus on innovation in engineering education and new pedagogical approaches.
author: Mary Beth Gallagher | School of Engineering

title: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
summary:
For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm.

At its annual
author: michael.nunez@venturebeat.com (Michael Nuñez)

title: Two from MIT named 2026 Knight-Hennessy Scholars
summary: The prestigious fellowship funds graduate studies at Stanford University.
author: Julia Mongo | Office of Distinguished Fellowships

title: Study: Firms often use automation to control certain workers’ wages
summary: MIT economists found US companies tend to target employees earning a “wage premium,” which increases inequality but not necessarily productivity.
author: Peter Dizikes | MIT News

title: Beacon Biosignals is mapping the brain during sleep
summary: Founded by Jake Donoghue PhD ’19 and former MIT researcher Jarrett Revels, the company is creating an AI-driven platform to help diagnose and treat disease.
author: Zach Winn | MIT News

title: Making the case for curiosity-driven science
summary: President Sally Kornbluth spoke in front of a packed crowd about growing challenges to the U.S. research ecosystem as funding for America’s top research universities becomes increasingly strained.
author: MIT News

title: The MIT-IBM Computing Research Lab launches to shape the future of AI and quantum computing
summary: Building on a long-standing MIT–IBM collaboration, the new lab will chart the convergence of AI, algorithms, and quantum computing.
author: MIT Schwarzman College of Computing

title: Enabling privacy-preserving AI training on everyday devices
summary: A new method could bring more accurate and efficient AI models to high-stakes applications like health care and finance, even in under-resourced settings.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
